# Welcome Aaron Srivastava - Director, Developer Platform & AI Enablement (Exchange)

Dear Colleagues,

Today I am very excited to welcome Aaron Srivastava to the Exchange team. He joins us as Director, Developer Platform & AI Enablement, bringing extensive experience in AI-native software development, AI-assisted development tooling and processes, and DevOps practices including CI/CD and AI integration throughout the software development lifecycle. See slightly extended bio below.

Aaron will work cross-functionally within Exchange to help us move towards a truly AI-native software development process and drive increases in engineering productivity and delivery speed.

Please help me make him feel welcome to the team!

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Aaron is a software engineer at heart and has been working in the healthcare industry since college. Most recently, he worked at Nevro (now part of Globus Medical), where he began as a software engineer over seven years ago and eventually served as Director of IT Infrastructure and Cloud Engineering. In that role, he led initiatives across DevSecOps, cloud platform architecture, identity and endpoint management, and engineering enablement. Prior to Nevro, he worked as a software engineer at PrecisionLender and earlier at Fujifilm Medical Systems, focusing on healthcare and enterprise software development. He holds a master’s degree in Engineering Management from Johns Hopkins University and earned his undergraduate degree in Biomedical Engineering from North Carolina State University.

He lives in North Carolina with his wife, their dog Brandy, and their cat Artemis. Outside of work, Aaron spends much of his time building things—both technical and physical. He enjoys working on home projects, running a personal home lab, experimenting with AI and self-hosted infrastructure, and planning travel whenever he gets the chance. Many of his hobbies tend to evolve into projects, whether that means renovating something, automating it, or figuring out how it works under the hood.
